mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate cuts (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that immediately depress equity valuations and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate earnings growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to yield a positive 60-day return direction in > 60% of observations, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation indicate supply-side bottlenecks that force monetary tightening, causing immediate negative market repricing over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or neutral when industrial production declines during high inflation periods, with a hit rate below 45% over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicate real rate tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between inflation and policy rates shows a positive or null relationship with 20-day returns, or the combined signal yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 50%, or the rank correlation between payroll changes and forward returns drops below 0.04 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 8 percent signals a sudden collapse in construction demand and consumer confidence, triggering immediate risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in > 58% of instances following an 8% housing starts drop, or the threshold signal shows no predictive power (p > 0.05) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment during economic recoveries indicates robust capacity utilization and labor absorption, which fuels equity bull markets and positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising production and falling unemployment fails to generate positive 20-day returns in > 55% of recovery periods, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant.
